summary: Clock initialization fix verified - Football games now correctly initialize clock to game's period_duration setting instead of hardcoded 15:00 (900 seconds). All tests passed.
seed_data_creation: Created test games with different period_duration values (480s, 600s, 720s)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Clock initialization fix is working correctly. The fix in FootballLiveGame.jsx (lines 858-892) now reads period_duration from game object and initializes clock accordingly. Quarter advancement (lines 2144-2151) also correctly resets clock to game's period_duration.

clock_init_480_seconds.description: Game with period_duration=480 (8:00) correctly shows 8:00 clock

clock_init_600_seconds.description: Game with period_duration=600 (10:00) correctly shows 10:00 clock

clock_init_720_seconds.description: Game with period_duration=720 (12:00) correctly shows 12:00 clock

quarter_advancement_clock_reset.description: Advancing quarter resets clock to game's period_duration (8:00 for 480s game)

clock_persistence.description: Clock time persists correctly when saving and reloading game state

api_period_duration.description: API correctly returns period_duration in game response

test_flow: 1. Backend API tests: Verified period_duration is stored and returned correctly
2. Created new game with 10:00 period duration - clock showed 10:00
3. Loaded existing game with 8:00 period duration - clock showed 8:00

code_changes_verified.fetchGame_function: Lines 858-892: Now uses res.data.period_duration to initialize clock
code_changes_verified.handleAdvanceQuarter: Lines 2144-2151: Now uses game?.period_duration to reset clock
